title: Leonia ulei
taxonomy: Leonia ulei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Calophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Leonia, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions of the Americas. The species is a type of small tree or shrub, typically found in humid and shaded areas. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family and genus. Within the botanical community, Leonia ulei is recognized as a distinct species based on its unique morphological characteristics.

intro: Leonia ulei, also known as "uva ursi of the Amazon", is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with benefits including the treatment of urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Leonia ulei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leonia ulei is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and decoctions, as well as dried leaf extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
